title: Lepturus pulchellus
intro: Lepturus pulchellus, also known as purple millet or purple grass, is a type of annual grass native to tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include reducing inflammation and improving respiratory health. The bioactive constituents of Lepturus pulchellus include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its pharmacological activities. Lepturus pulchellus is commonly used in herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, to alleviate symptoms of coughs and colds, as well as to support immune function.
taxonomy: Lepturus pulchellus belongs to the family Poaceae, commonly known as the grass family, and is classified as a type of grass.
